title: Discrete-Time Complex Exponential e^{jΩn}
summary: This page introduces the discrete-time complex exponential and relates it to cosine and sine through Euler’s formula, emphasizing its frequency, magnitude, and angle interpretation on the unit circle. It also begins Example 3.5, showing MATLAB code to plot a discrete-time sinusoid x[n] = cos((π/12)n + π/4), and includes a footnote about special properties of discrete-time sinusoids such as periodicity and bandlimiting.
